title: Dropping Referential Integrity in the Database
breadcrumbs: Dropping Referential Integrity in the Database
text: Dropping Referential Integrity in the Database If you have implemented database referential control and you want to revert to having Uniface do that, you can generate and run an SQL script to drop referential integrity. Generate the SQL scripts for dropping referential integrity. See Generate Referential Integrity Scripts . Configure the connector to enable Uniface referential integrity by adding or removing the relevant connector option. See the connector documentation for your DBMS. Execute the scripts to drop referential integrity constraints ( *_dropReferentialIntegrity.sql ). Load the data into the tables. Run the script to validate relationship constraints ( ( *_validateReferentialIntegrity.sql ).). Investigate and resolve any reported violations of referential integrity. Execute the script to enforce referential integrity constraints ( *_createReferentialIntegrity.sql ). Configure the connector for database referential integrity support again. For more information, see Generate Referential Integrity Scripts . Edit the application assignment file to configure the connector to enable Uniface referential integrity. You will need to add or remove the relevant connector option. See the connector documentation for your DBMS. Edit the resulting SQL script to specify your database name. Execute the script to drop referential integrity constraints ( *_dropReferentialIntegrity.sql ). Configure the connector for Uniface referential integrity support. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
